data.text: Cricket-India thump Zimbabwe to revive T20 World Cup title defence
data.snippet: A rampant India piled up a record total and handed Zimbabwe a 72-run shellacking in a Super Eight contest on Thursday to revive their Twenty20 World Cup title defence.

image.text: India's Hardik Pandya celebrates with Tilak Varma after reaching his half century during ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026 Super 8 match between India and Zimbabwe at MA Chidambaram Stadium, Chennai, India on February 26, 2026 .

relatedHeadlines.text: Cricket-South Africa close in on T20 World Cup semis with nine-wicket win over West Indies
relatedHeadlines.snippet: South Africa are on the verge of securing their place in the semi-finals of the Twenty20 World Cup after captain Aiden Markram's unbeaten knock of 82 led them to a commanding nine-wicket win over West Indies on Thursday.

relatedHeadlines.text: Cricket-New Zealand want to leave nothing to chance in battle to reach T20 World Cup semis
relatedHeadlines.snippet: New Zealand head into Friday’s showdown with England knowing their Twenty20 World Cup fate hangs by a thread, with a win the only way to avoid leaving their semi-final hopes at the mercy of rivals and net run rates.

relatedHeadlines.text: Cricket-Sri Lanka's Shanaka says constant criticism has affected players' mental health
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Sri Lanka captain Dasun Shanaka said the relentless criticism aimed at his players has been damaging to their mental health and undermined their efforts at the Twenty20 World Cup, where they were eliminated from the group stage on Wednesday.

data.text: PREVIEW-Soccer-Dortmund desperate to cut Bayern's lead and stay in title contention
data.snippet: Borussia Dortmund are running out of chances to land any silverware this season and their Bundesliga match against visitors Bayern Munich on Saturday could be their last opportunity to close the gap to the league leaders and stay in title contention.

image.text: Borussia Dortmund's Nico Schlotterbeck in action with Bayern Munich's Harry Kane during the Bundesliga match at Allianz Arena, Munich, Germany, October 18, 2025.

data.text: Soccer-Ronaldo buys 25% stake in Spanish second-tier club Almeria
data.snippet: Cristiano Ronaldo has acquired a 25% stake in Spanish second-tier side Almeria, the club said on Thursday.

image.text: Al Nassr's Cristiano Ronaldo reacts during the Saudi Pro League match against Al Kholood at King Abdullah Sport City Stadium, Buraydah, Saudi Arabia, January 30, 2026.

data.text: Motor racing-Netflix and Apple TV join forces on F1 content
data.snippet: Netflix will broadcast the Canadian Formula One Grand Prix live to U.S. viewers in May as part of a deal that makes season eight of its hit docu-series 'Drive to Survive' available for streaming on Apple TV.

image.text: A drone view shows the Netflix logo on one of their buildings in the Hollywood neighborhood of Los Angeles, California, December 8, 2025.

data.text: English Premier League to debut streaming service in Singapore, FT reports
data.snippet: The world’s most popular club football competition, the English Premier League, is set to launch its own direct-to-consumer streaming service in Singapore this year, with plans for potential expansion to other countries, the Financial Times reported on Thursday.

image.text: Microphone showing an image of the premier league logo during Premier League between Watford and Tottenham Hotspur at Vicarage Road, Watford, Britain on January 1, 2022.

data.text: NFL-'Are you a dog or a cat?' Welcome to the NFL Scouting Combine
data.snippet: Over the next four days in Indianapolis, 319 elite prospects will sprint, jump, lift weights and field bizarre questions at the NFL Scouting Combine, a televised audition before league decision-makers and a media throng rivaled only by the Super Bowl.

image.text: exas tight end Jack Endries (TE06) speaks to members of the media during the NFL Combine at the Indiana Convention Center in  Indianapolis, IN, USA on Feb 26, 2026.

data.text: Ice hockey-PWHL reaping the benefits of Olympic women's tournament
data.snippet: The Professional Women's Hockey League (PWHL) is poised to capitalise on increased interest in the sport with players returning to the rink with their clubs following the conclusion of the Milano Cortina Olympics.

image.text: Gold medallists United States pose for a team group photo during the Milano Cortina 2026 Olympics  Ice Hockey Women's Victory Ceremony at Milano Santagiulia Ice Hockey Arena, Milan, Italy, February 19, 2026.

data.text: Badminton-France eyes Olympic push as long rebuild starts to pay off
data.snippet: French badminton is targeting future Olympic success with Christo Popov's World Tour Finals victory and a landmark European team crown marking what officials believe could be a potential turning point for the sport in the country.

image.text: France's Christo Popov in action during his India Open 2026 quarter final match against Indonesia's Jonatan Christie at Indira Gandhi Indoor Stadium, New Delhi, India, January 16, 2026.

data.text: Sailing-Palanad 4 holds nerve to clinch IRC Zero crown in Caribbean 600
data.snippet: Antoine Magre's Palanad 4 secured IRC Zero victory in a Caribbean 600 race decided by tactical nerves and sustained speed, posting the best corrected time after two days, 10 hours and 32 minutes of intense sailing.

data.text: Athletics-Welteji banned for two years by CAS for doping violation
data.snippet: Ethiopian middle-distance runner Diribe Welteji has been banned for two years by the Court of Arbitration for Sport after failing to provide a doping sample in February 2025.

image.text: Diribe Welteji of Ethiopia reacts after winning her semi final during Paris 2024 Olympics Athletics Women's 1500m Semi-Final 2 at Stade de France, Saint-Denis, France on August 08, 2024.

data.text: EXCLUSIVE-Blitzer joins bidding war for Indian Premier League cricket teams, sources say
data.snippet: Billionaire David Blitzer is eyeing a majority stake in an Indian Premier League cricket team and is holding talks with two franchises, two sources said, stoking a bidding war against Manchester United co-chairman Avram Glazer and Indian tycoons.

image.text: Royal Challengers Bengaluru players celebrate with the trophy after winning the Indian Premier League Final match between Royal Challengers Bengaluru v Punjab Kings at Narendra Modi Stadium, Ahmedabad, India, June 4, 2025.

relatedHeadlines.text: Cricket-J&K captain downplays headbutt in India's Ranji Trophy
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Jammu and Kashmir captain Paras Dogra looked to take the sting out of a headbutting incident during the second day of India's Ranji Trophy final after he rammed his helmet into that of a Karnataka fielder following a heated exchange on Wednesday.

data.text: Soccer-PSG have been toughened up for later stages of the Champions League - Luis Enrique
data.snippet: Champions League holders Paris Saint-Germain have fought through a tougher schedule than any other side to reach the last 16 in this season’s competition, coach Luis Enrique said after they squeezed past Monaco on Wednesday.

image.text: Paris St Germain coach Luis Enrique before the UEFA Champions League First Leg Play Off match against AS Monaco at Stade Louis II, Monaco, February 17, 2026.

relatedHeadlines.text: Soccer-Italy's sole survivor in Champions League cannot mask Serie A struggles
relatedHeadlines.snippet: For the second successive season, Serie A has one club remaining in the last 16 of the Champions League as Italian football struggles to compete in Europe's premier club competition and risks falling even further behind.

relatedHeadlines.text: Soccer-Galatasaray advance after extra time to deny stunning Juventus comeback
Soccer-Vinicius scores as Real Madrid reach Champions League last 16
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Aurelien Tchouameni and Vinicius Jr. scored to help Real Madrid fight back for a 2-1 win over Benfica on Wednesday and book their Champions League last-16 spot with a 3-1 aggregate win in the knockout round playoff.

data.text: PREVIEW-Soccer-Australia look to revive 'Matildas mania' in Women's Asian Cup
data.snippet: Hosts Australia will hope to revive the 'Matildas mania' that swept the nation during their run to the 2023 World Cup semi-finals as they bid for a second Women's Asian Cup title.

image.text: Australia's Sam Kerr and teammates applaud fans after the match after the FIFA Women's World Cup Australia and New Zealand 2023 Quarter Final match between Australia v France at Brisbane Stadium, Brisbane, Australia, August 12, 2023.

relatedHeadlines.text: Soccer-Kerr to captain Australia in Women's Asian Cup, Fowler returns
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Sam Kerr will lead Australia's bid for a second Women's Asian Cup title with forward Mary Fowler making her long-awaited return to the hosts' squad for the continental showpiece.

data.text: Rugby-New Zealand's Woodman-Wickliffe retires for second time
data.snippet: New Zealand try-scoring machine Portia Woodman-Wickliffe has announced her second retirement from international rugby after coming back to play in her third Women's World Cup last year.

image.text: New Zealand's Portia Woodman-Wickliffe in action with Japan's Komachi Imakugi during the Women's World Cup 2025 Pool C match between New Zealand v Japan at Sandy Park, Exeter, Britain, August 31, 2025.

relatedHeadlines.text: Rugby-Ireland wing Lowe out of rest of Six Nations with groin injury
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Ireland will be without experienced wing James Lowe for the remainder of the Six Nations Championship after he was ruled out with a groin injury, officials said on Thursday.

relatedHeadlines.text: Rugby-England scrumhalf Mitchell ruled out of Six Nations - BBC
relatedHeadlines.snippet: England's Six Nations campaign suffered a major blow when scrumhalf Alex Mitchell was ruled out of the rest of the tournament with a hamstring injury sustained in Saturday's 42-21 defeat by Ireland, the BBC reported on Wednesday.

data.text: Tennis-Austin WTA tournament sets up 'rage room' for players to blow off steam
data.snippet: A WTA tournament in Texas has unveiled a "rage room" for players to take out their frustrations away from the cameras after Coco Gauff's racket-smashing outburst at the Australian Open last month sparked debate about privacy at tournaments.

image.text: The smashed racket of Denmark's Holger Rune is pictured during his Japan Open semi final match against France's Arthur Fils at Ariake Tennis Forest Park, Tokyo, Japan, September 30, 2024.

relatedHeadlines.text: Tennis-'Are we animals in the zoo?': Swiatek calls for player privacy at Australian Open
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Iga Swiatek and Novak Djokovic joined a growing chorus of players demanding more privacy off the courts at the Australian Open after cameras captured Coco Gauff in a post-match meltdown that the American said should have been a personal moment.

relatedHeadlines.text: Tennis-Gauff unhappy with Australian Open broadcasting video of her smashing racket after loss
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Coco Gauff looked for a place without cameras to channel her frustration after a straight-sets loss to Elina Svitolina in the Australian Open quarter-finals, but was unhappy to find out that a video of her smashing her racket on the floor was broadcast to viewers worldwide.

notifications.message: Your requested language with value en-GB is not supported, using en-us instead.
